title: Modern Bunk Beds (Side Street)
summary: A twin-over-twin bunk bed with solid 2x6-slab ends and bolted siderails — a chunkier, more modern-looking frame than this catalog's other bunk designs, with an optional guardrail and ladder.
description:
This bunk bed builds its end panels from solid glued-up 2x6 slabs rather than a frame-and-panel or simple post design, giving it a chunky, modern block look that's genuinely distinct from this catalog's other bunk beds. Glue up each end panel from edge-jointed 2x6 boards clamped tightly while curing — this is the one step in the whole build where clamp pressure and glue-up time matter more than any measurement.

Once the end panels are cut to their final shape, bore bolt holes through them for the siderails using bed rail hardware — **bolted, not screwed, connections carry a sleeping load and need to resist loosening over years of use in a way wood screws alone don't.** Attach the siderails to the end panels with bed bolts and cross supports to carry the mattress slats.

The guardrail on the top bunk and the ladder are both optional but strongly recommended for any household with young kids climbing to the top bunk. Slats span the width of the frame at the bottom and top to support each mattress directly, with no separate box spring needed.

tools.note: Gluing up the solid end panels.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Glued edge to edge in groups of six to form four end panels.

steps.title: Glue up the end panels
steps.body: Edge-glue six 2x6 boards together per end panel, clamping tightly and letting cure fully. Build four end panels total (two per bed level pair).

steps.title: Shape and cut the end panels
steps.body: Once cured, cut each end panel to final height and optionally shape the top profile with a jigsaw. Sand smooth.

steps.title: Bore bolt holes and attach siderails
steps.body: Bore bolt holes through the end panels for the bed rail hardware. Attach the siderails to the end panels with bed bolts.

steps.title: Add cross supports and slats
steps.body: Attach cross supports across the siderails with 3" screws to carry the mattress slats, then lay and attach the slats.

steps.title: Add the guardrail and ladder
steps.body: Attach the guardrail posts and guardrail to the top bunk's open side. Build the ladder from the rails and rungs and attach to the end of the bed.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Modern bunk bed with chunky solid end panels
